PRELIMINARY STORM SURVEY BY A NWS TEAM FOUND DAMAGE ASSOCIATED WITH AN EF2 FROM LEIGHTON TO ROGERSVILLE, ALABAMA. THE PATH LENGTH OF THIS TORNADO WAS ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 9.6 MILES.

PRELIMINARY STORM SURVEY FROM A NWS TEAM FOUND DAMAGE FROM AN EF1 TORNADO IN THREE DISTINCT...THOUGH NOT CONTINUOUS...SEGMENTS FROM SOUTHWEST TO NORTHEAST OF THE CITY OF CULLMAN. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. MAJOR STRUCTRUAL DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED NORTHEAST OF CULLMAN. THE PATH LENGTH OF THIS TORNADO WAS ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 3 MILES IN AND AROUND SMITH LAKE...7 MILES FROM SULPHER SPRINGS TO TRIMBLE...AND 5 MILES FROM EAST OF CULLMAN TO SIMCOE.
